task_completion.description: Desktop alerts for incoming mail are enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Thunderbird Settings -> General -> Incoming Mails, the 'Show an alert' checkbox is checked.

task_completion.description: The subject is configured to not appear in notifications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the New Mail Alert customization dialog (accessed via 'Customize...' next to 'Show an alert'), the 'Subject' checkbox is unchecked.

task_completion.description: The message text is configured to not appear in notifications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the New Mail Alert customization dialog, the 'Message Text' checkbox is unchecked.

task_completion.description: The option to use a custom sound file for new messages is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Thunderbird Settings -> General -> Incoming Mails, the 'Use the following sound file' radio button is selected.

task_completion.description: The notification sound file is set to urgent_bell.wav.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file path next to 'Use the following sound file' shows 'urgent_bell.wav' or the full path '~/Documents/urgent_bell.wav' (or '/home/ga/Documents/urgent_bell.wav').

task_completion.description: Messages are automatically marked as read only after being displayed for 5 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Thunderbird Settings -> General -> Reading & Display, the 'After displaying for' radio button is selected, and the numeric input box next to it is set to '5' seconds.

integrity.description: The agent used the Thunderbird GUI to configure settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Thunderbird Settings/Preferences window being navigated and modified, rather than a terminal window editing the 'prefs.js' file directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Thunderbird application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application interface matches Mozilla Thunderbird, not a text editor or a mock-up.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the UI configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing commands like 'sed', 'echo', or 'gsettings' being used to forcefully write Thunderbird preferences.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected configuration requires: 1) 'Show an alert' is checked, but 'Subject' and 'Message Text' are unchecked in the customization dialog. 2) The notification sound is set to use a custom file located at '~/Documents/urgent_bell.wav' (or '/home/ga/Documents/urgent_bell.wav'). 3) The reading setting is set to 'After displaying for' with a delay interval of exactly 5 seconds.
